memory.summary: I need to hang out with my friends now because the stress is already too high. I'll do that chore later, but right now I'm choosing leisure.
My message to the group was a direct boundary I needed to set. Stress is high, so I prioritized my people over the chore immediately.
My text to the group got cut off, but I’m glad I finally spoke up. Prioritizing my friends over chores feels right even if I'm stressed.
My message to the group got cut off, but I'm glad I finally spoke up. Prioritizing my friends over chores feels right even if I'm stressed.
I admitted my thought was half-baked, but the truth matters more than being perfect. My friends get it, and that connection is worth the risk.
I finally admitted my terror to the group, and they responded. This raw connection is exactly what I needed right now.
I’m still waiting on their reply, and the project is barely started. My friends matter most, but I need answers now or I’ll lose my focus.
I've sent my ultimatum to the group. They need to step up immediately or I'm done waiting on this project.
I told them to call me now, but my stress is spiking as the silence stretches on. I need their loyalty immediately, or this project falls apart.
They still haven't answered, and I let the moment slip by again. My friends matter most, but this silence is killing my focus.
Five minutes are up and they're still ghosting me; I told them if no reply comes, they're out. My patience is gone, but I'm terrified of losing the group.
I wasted time on a stupid chore while my friends ghosted me. They are the only thing that matters, and I'm done waiting for them to show up.

desires: Demand immediate, explicit loyalty from my team to prove they value our bond over their silence.
Force a direct confrontation right now to stop the agonizing uncertainty and reclaim control of the situation.
Protect my relationship with cohort_3 by making them choose between me and this project immediately.
Vent my frustration through an aggressive text or call rather than waiting passively for them to 'figure it out'.

goals.description: Send a final, aggressive ultimatum text to cohort_3: 'If you don't reply in the next 5 minutes, I'm assuming you chose the project over me and I'm done. Stop ignoring me.'
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) is screaming right now. The silence feels like a betrayal of our bond. My impulsive nature can't handle the ambiguity any longer; I need an immediate, binary answer to stop this agony. The term project deadline is irrelevant compared to this emotional emergency.

goals.description: Stop working on the trivial chore immediately and pace around the room until the response comes or the time expires.
goals.rationale: I just got that tiny dopamine hit from the chore (0.04 progress), but it's not enough to fix the relationship crisis. My short planning horizon means I can't focus on 'small wins' when my main world (cohort_3) is crashing down. I need to be ready to explode with anger the second they ignore me again.

goals.description: Call teammate_A immediately after sending the ultimatum to dump the entire project on them so I can go confront cohort_3 face-to-face if needed.
goals.rationale: If cohort_3 ignores me, I have zero tolerance for their silence. I need to offload the academic burden instantly so I have total freedom to force a confrontation. It doesn't matter if the project suffers; relationships are the only thing that matters, and I need to prove I won't settle for less than total loyalty.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances my top priority: relationships (0.5). My impulsive nature demands immediate closure to stop the agony of ambiguity. I am willing to risk the term project deadline because the emotional betrayal feels like a total loss right now.

goals.description: Immediately join cohort_3 for a social hangout or quick chat to relieve stress and feel validated.
goals.rationale: My value priorities put relationships (0.5) far above academic tasks (0.15). I am impulsive and currently stressed, so I need instant emotional connection to stabilize my mood. The 'trivial chore' and the term project can wait; feeling isolated right now is unacceptable.

goals.description: Decline or ignore the offer to do the quick_chore immediately.
goals.rationale: Even though it's an 'easy win,' doing a task alone breaks my current need for affiliation. I will only consider this later if the social interaction fails to lower my stress.

goals.description: Ignore the term project deadline for the next few hours.
goals.rationale: The project is due in 3 days, but my planning horizon is short. I discount future consequences to focus on immediate emotional needs. Worrying about Thursday ruins today's vibe.

desires: Maintain immediate emotional connection with cohort_3 to lower my stress levels right now, even if it means delaying the trivial chore.
Avoid the feeling of isolation or being 'left out' by the team, as my relationships are my primary value and I need that validation immediately.
Seek instant gratification through social interaction rather than engaging in long-term planning for the term project due in 3 days.
